{{Infobox Waterfall |name = Taruhime Falls (多留姫の滝, 樽姫の滝) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Taruhime Falls view from Ryuei Bridge.jpg|300px|Taruhime Falls]] |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|36.5|N|138|12|56|E|type:landmark_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012). |location = [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Chino City]] Tamagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 120. |height = 12 |width = 9 |watercourse = [[Tenryu River]] system, Yanagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 140. |osm_zoom = 10 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Taruhime Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Taruhime Shrine 2.jpg|300px|Taruhime Shrine Main Hall]]
Main Hall |location = Nagano Prefecture, Chino City, Tamagawa"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20214/L0968824/ Mapion Directory: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|35.5|N|138|12|53.5|E}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21486113884&latitude=35.993205205672 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |deity = [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] |ranking = |established = |main_hall = |alternate_name = |pilgrimage = |festival = |rituals = }} '''Taruhime Falls''' (多留姫の滝, also 樽姫の滝) is a waterfall in [[Chino, Nagano|Chino City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as "Taruhime Falls" in alternate kanji‘Chino City History: Modern and Folklore,’ pp. 1069–1070.‘Chino City History: Supplement Natural History,’ p. 33.. The falls are designated as a cultural property by Chino City. == Geography == '''Taruhime Falls''' is located east of Chino City's central area, at the foot of [[Mount Oizumi]], along the [[Yanagawa (Nagano)|Yanagawa River]]. From the summit of Mount Oizumi, one can see [[Mount Fuji]] to the left of [[Mount Yatsugatake]], with the falls visible below‘Nagano Prefectural Town and Village Records: Chuo and Minami-Shin Regions,’ p. 3351.. The falls have an initial drop of 9 meters in height and width, followed by a right-angle turn with a 3-meter height and 1-meter width. The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[autumn leaves]], and [[snow]] in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Taruhime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Taruhime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Taruhime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Taruhime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Taruhime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Taruhime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Taruhime Shrine 1.jpg|Taruhime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Taruhime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Taruhime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説}} : A legend tells of Taruhime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Taruhime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Taruhime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Taruhime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Taruhime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from [[JR East]]'s [[Chuo Main Line]] [[Chino Station]], reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Taruhime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Taruhime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Taruhime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Taruhime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from [[JR East]]'s [[Chuo Main Line]] [[Chino Station]], reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. {{Infobox Waterfall |name = Taruhime Falls (多留姫の滝, 樽姫の滝) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Taruhime Falls view from Ryuei Bridge.jpg|300px|Taruhime Falls]] |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|36.5|N|138|12|56|E|type:landmark_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012). |location = [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Chino City]] Tamagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 120. |height = 12 |width = 9 |watercourse = [[Tenryu River]] system, Yanagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 140. |osm_zoom = 10 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Taruhime Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Taruhime Shrine 2.jpg|300px|Taruhime Shrine Main Hall]]
Main Hall |location = Nagano Prefecture, Chino City, Tamagawa"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20214/L0968824/ Mapion Directory: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|35.5|N|138|12|53.5|E}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21486113884&latitude=35.993205205672 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |deity = [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] |ranking = |established = |main_hall = |alternate_name = |pilgrimage = |festival = |rituals = }} '''Taruhime Falls''' (多留姫の滝, also 樽姫の滝) is a waterfall in [[Chino, Nagano|Chino City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as "Taruhime Falls" in alternate kanji‘Chino City History: Modern and Folklore,’ pp. 1069–1070.‘Chino City History: Supplement Natural History,’ p. 33.. The falls are designated as a cultural property by Chino City. == Geography == '''Taruhime Falls''' is located east of Chino City's central area, at the foot of [[Mount Oizumi]], along the [[Yanagawa (Nagano)|Yanagawa River]]. From the summit of Mount Oizumi, one can see [[Mount Fuji]] to the left of [[Mount Yatsugatake]], with the falls visible below‘Nagano Prefectural Town and Village Records: Chuo and Minami-Shin Regions,’ p. 3351.. The falls have an initial drop of 9 meters in height and width, followed by a right-angle turn with a 3-meter height and 1-meter width. The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[autumn leaves]], and [[snow]] in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Taruhime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Taruhime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Taruhime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Taruhime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Taruhime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Taruhime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Taruhime Shrine 1.jpg|Taruhime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Taruhime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Taruhime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説}} : A legend tells of Taruhime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Taruhime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Taruhime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Taruhime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Taruhime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from [[JR East]]'s [[Chuo Main Line]] [[Chino Station]], reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Taruhime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Taruhime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Taruhime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Taruhime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Taruhime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Taruhime Shrine 1.jpg|Taruhime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Taruhime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Taruhime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説}} : A legend tells of Taruhime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Taruhime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Taruhime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Taruhime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Taruhime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from [[JR East]]'s [[Chuo Main Line]] [[Chino Station]], reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. {{Infobox Waterfall |name = Taruhime Falls (多留姫の滝, 樽姫の滝) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Taruhime Falls view from Ryuei Bridge.jpg|300px|Taruhime Falls]] |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|36.5|N|138|12|56|E|type:landmark_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012). |location = [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Chino City]] Tamagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 120. |height = 12 |width = 9 |watercourse = [[Tenryu River]] system, Yanagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 140. |osm_zoom = 10 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Taruhime Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Taruhime Shrine 2.jpg|300px|Taruhime Shrine Main Hall]]
Main Hall |location = Nagano Prefecture, Chino City, Tamagawa"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20214/L0968824/ Mapion Directory: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|35.5|N|138|12|53.5|E}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21486113884&latitude=35.993205205672 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |deity = [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] |ranking = |established = |main_hall = |alternate_name = |pilgrimage = |festival = |rituals = }} '''Taruhime Falls''' (多留姫の滝, also 樽姫の滝) is a waterfall in [[Chino, Nagano|Chino City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as "Taruhime Falls" in alternate kanji‘Chino City History: Modern and Folklore,’ pp. 1069–1070.‘Chino City History: Supplement Natural History,’ p. 33.. The falls are designated as a cultural property by Chino City. == Geography == '''Taruhime Falls''' is located east of Chino City's central area, at the foot of [[Mount Oizumi]], along the [[Yanagawa (Nagano)|Yanagawa River]]. From the summit of Mount Oizumi, one can see [[Mount Fuji]] to the left of [[Mount Yatsugatake]], with the falls visible below‘Nagano Prefectural Town and Village Records: Chuo and Minami-Shin Regions,’ p. 3351.. The falls have an initial drop of 9 meters in height and width, followed by a right-angle turn with a 3-meter height and 1-meter width. The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[autumn leaves]], and [[snow]] in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Taruhime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Taruhime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Taruhime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Taruhime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Taruhime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Taruhime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Taruhime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. {{Infobox Waterfall |name = Taruhime Falls (多留姫の滝, 樽姫の滝) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Taruhime Falls view from Ryuei Bridge.jpg|300px|Taruhime Falls]] |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|36.5|N|138|12|56|E|type:landmark_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012). |location = [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Chino City]] Tamagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 120. |height = 12 |width = 9 |watercourse = [[Tenryu River]] system, Yanagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 140. |osm_zoom = 10 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Taruhime Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Taruhime Shrine 2.jpg|300px|Taruhime Shrine Main Hall]]
Main Hall |location = Nagano Prefecture, Chino City, Tamagawa"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20214/L0968824/ Mapion Directory: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|35.5|N|138|12|53.5|E}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21486113884&latitude=35.993205205672 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |deity = [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] |ranking = |established = |main_hall = |alternate_name = |pilgrimage = |festival = |rituals = }} '''Taruhime Falls''' (多留姫の滝, also 樽姫の滝) is a waterfall in [[Chino, Nagano|Chino City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as "Taruhime Falls" in alternate kanji‘Chino City History: Modern and Folklore,’ pp. 1069–1070.‘Chino City History: Supplement Natural History,’ p. 33.. The falls are designated as a cultural property by Chino City. == Geography == '''Taruhime Falls''' is located east of Chino City's central area, at the foot of [[Mount Oizumi]], along the [[Yanagawa (Nagano)|Yanagawa River]]. From the summit of Mount Oizumi, one can see [[Mount Fuji]] to the left of [[Mount Yatsugatake]], with the falls visible below‘Nagano Prefectural Town and Village Records: Chuo and Minami-Shin Regions,’ p. 3351.. The falls have an initial drop of 9 meters in height and width, followed by a right-angle turn with a 3-meter height and 1-meter width. The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[autumn leaves]], and [[snow]] in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Taruhime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Taruhime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Taruhime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Taruhime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Taruhime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Taruhime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Taruhime Shrine 1.jpg|Taruhime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Taruhime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Taruhime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説}} : A legend tells of Taruhime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Taruhime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Taruhime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Taruhime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Taruhime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from [[JR East]]'s [[Chuo Main Line]] [[Chino Station]], reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from [[JR East]]'s [[Chuo Main Line]] [[Chino Station]], reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. revision.text:
{{Infobox Waterfall |name = Taruhime Falls (多留姫の滝, 樽姫の滝) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Taruhime Falls view from Ryuei Bridge.jpg|300px|Taruhime Falls]] |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|36.5|N|138|12|56|E|type:landmark_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012). |location = [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Chino City]] Tamagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 120. |height = 12 |width = 9 |watercourse = [[Tenryu River]] system, Yanagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 140. |osm_zoom = 10 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Taruhime Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Taruhime Shrine 2.jpg|300px|Taruhime Shrine Main Hall]]
Main Hall |location = Nagano Prefecture, Chino City, Tamagawa"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20214/L0968824/ Mapion Directory: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|35.5|N|138|12|53.5|E}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21486113884&latitude=35.993205205672 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |deity = [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] |ranking = |established = |main_hall = |alternate_name = |pilgrimage = |festival = |rituals = }} '''Taruhime Falls''' (多留姫の滝, also 樽姫の滝) is a waterfall in [[Chino, Nagano|Chino City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as "Taruhime Falls" in alternate kanji‘Chino City History: Modern and Folklore,’ pp. 1069–1070.‘Chino City History: Supplement Natural History,’ p. 33.. The falls are designated as a cultural property by Chino City. == Geography == '''Taruhime Falls''' is located east of Chino City's central area, at the foot of [[Mount Oizumi]], along the [[Yanagawa (Nagano)|Yanagawa River]]. From the summit of Mount Oizumi, one can see [[Mount Fuji]] to the left of [[Mount Yatsugatake]], with the falls visible below‘Nagano Prefectural Town and Village Records: Chuo and Minami-Shin Regions,’ p. 3351.. The falls have an initial drop of 9 meters in height and width, followed by a right-angle turn with a 3-meter height and 1-meter width. The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[autumn leaves]], and [[snow]] in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Taruhime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Taruhime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Taruhime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Taruhime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Taruhime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Taruhime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Taruhime Shrine 1.jpg|Taruhime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Taruhime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Taruhime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説}} : A legend tells of Taruhime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Taruhime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Taruhime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Taruhime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Taruhime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from [[JR East]]'s [[Chuo Main Line]] [[Chino Station]], reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Taruhime's Waterfall]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[sn:Taruhime's Waterfall]] [[en:Taruhime's Waterfall]]

Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from [[JR East]]'s [[Chuo Main Line]] [[Chino Station]], reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Taruhime's Waterfall]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[sn:Taruhime's Waterfall]] [[en:Taruhime's Waterfall]]

{{Infobox Waterfall |name = Taruhime Falls (多留姫の滝, 樽姫の滝) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Taruhime Falls view from Ryuei Bridge.jpg|300px|Taruhime Falls]] |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|36.5|N|138|12|56|E|type:landmark_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012). |location = [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Chino City]] Tamagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 120. |height = 12 |width = 9 |watercourse = [[Tenryu River]] system, Yanagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 140. |osm_zoom = 10 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Taruhime Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Taruhime Shrine 2.jpg|300px|Taruhime Shrine Main Hall]]
Main Hall |location = Nagano Prefecture, Chino City, Tamagawa"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20214/L0968824/ Mapion Directory: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|35.5|N|138|12|53.5|E}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21486113884&latitude=35.993205205672 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |deity = [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] |ranking = |established = |main_hall = |alternate_name = |pilgrimage = |festival = |rituals = }} '''Taruhime Falls''' (多留姫の滝, also 樽姫の滝) is a waterfall in [[Chino, Nagano|Chino City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as "Taruhime Falls" in alternate kanji‘Chino City History: Modern and Folklore,’ pp. 1069–1070.‘Chino City History: Supplement Natural History,’ p. 33.. The falls are designated as a cultural property by Chino City. == Geography == '''Taruhime Falls''' is located east of Chino City's central area, at the foot of [[Mount Oizumi]], along the [[Yanagawa (Nagano)|Yanagawa River]]. From the summit of Mount Oizumi, one can see [[Mount Fuji]] to the left of [[Mount Yatsugatake]], with the falls visible below‘Nagano Prefectural Town and Village Records: Chuo and Minami-Shin Regions,’ p. 3351.. The falls have an initial drop of 9 meters in height and width, followed by a right-angle turn with a 3-meter height and 1-meter width. The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[autumn leaves]], and [[snow]] in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Taruhime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Taruhime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Taruhime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Taruhime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Taruhime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Taruhime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Taruhime Shrine 1.jpg|Taruhime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Taruhime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Taruhime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説|12=draft}} : A legend tells of Taruhime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史|12=draft}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Taruhime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Taruhime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Taruhime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Taruhime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from [[JR East]]'s [[Chuo Main Line]] [[Chino Station]], reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Taruhime's Waterfall]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[sn:Taruhime's Waterfall]] [[en:Taruhime's Waterfall]]

The falls have an initial drop of 9 meters in height and width, followed by a right-angle turn with a 3-meter height and 1-meter width. The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[autumn leaves]], and [[snow]] in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Taruhime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Taruhime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Taruhime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Taruhime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Taruhime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Taruhime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Taruhime Shrine 1.jpg|Taruhime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Taruhime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Taruhime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説|12=draft|12=draft}} : A legend tells of Taruhime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史|12=draft|12=draft}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Taruhime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Taruhime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Taruhime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Taruhime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from [[JR East]]'s [[Chuo Main Line]] [[Chino Station]], reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Taruhime's Waterfall]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[sn:Taruhime's Waterfall]] [[en:Taruhime's Waterfall]]

Main Hall |location = Nagano Prefecture, Chino City, Tamagawa"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20214/L0968824/ Mapion Directory: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|35.5|N|138|12|53.5|E}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21486113884&latitude=35.993205205672 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |deity = [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] |Ranking = |established = |main_hall = |alternate_name = |pilgrimage = |festival = |rituals = }} '''Taruhime Falls''' (多留姫の滝, also 樽姫の滝) is a waterfall in [[Chino, Nagano|Chino City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as "Taruhime Falls" in alternate kanji‘Chino City History: Modern and Folklore,’ pp. 1069–1070.‘Chino City History: Supplement Natural History,’ p. 33.. The falls are designated as a cultural property by Chino City. == Geography == '''Taruhime Falls''' is located east of Chino City's central area, at the foot of [[Mount Oizumi]], along the [[Yanagawa (Nagano)|Yanagawa River]]. From the summit of Mount Oizumi, one can see [[Mount Fuji]] to the left of [[Mount Yatsugatake]], with the falls visible below‘Nagano Prefectural Town and Village Records: Chuo and Minami-Shin Regions,’ p. 3351.. The falls have an initial drop of 9 meters in height and width, followed by a right-angle turn with a 3-meter height and 1-meter width. The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[autumn leaves]], and [[snow]] in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Taruhime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Taruhime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Taruhime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Taruhime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Taruhime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Taruhime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史|12=draft|12=draft}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Taruhime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Taruhime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Taruhime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Taruhime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Taruhime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Taruhime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Taruhime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from [[JR East]]'s [[Chuo Main Line]] [[Chino Station]], reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Taruhime's Waterfall]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[sn:Taruhime's Waterfall]] [[en:Taruhime's Waterfall]]

revision.comment: 28 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:多留姫の滝 up to rev 97968335
revision.text:
{{Infobox 滝 |名称 = 多留姫の滝（樽姫の滝） |画像 = [[File:Taruhime Falls view from Ryuei Bridge.jpg|300px|多留姫の滝]] |座標 ={{Coord|35|59|36.5|N|138|12|56|E|type:landmark_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}}座標は「[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 ウォッちず]」（2012年5月26日閲覧）にて得た（参考値）。 |所在地 = [[長野県]][[茅野市]]玉川『信州の滝紀行 名瀑100選』120ページ。 |落差 = 12 |滝幅 = 9 |水系 = [[天竜川]][[水系]]柳川『信州の滝紀行 名瀑100選』140ページ。 |OSMズーム = 10 }} {{神社 |名称 = 多留姫神社 |画像 = [[File:Taruhime Shrine 2.jpg|300px|多留姫神社本殿]]
本殿 |所在地 = 長野県茅野市玉川「[http://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20214/L0968824/ Mapion 電話帳 多留姫神社]」より（2012年5月31日閲覧）。 |位置 = {{Coord|35|59|35.5|N|138|12|53.5|E}}座標は「[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21486113884&latitude=35.993205205672 ウォッちず]」（2012年5月31日閲覧）にて得た（参考値）。 |祭神 = [[多満留姫|多留姫神]] |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''多留姫の滝'''（たるひめのたき）は、[[長野県]][[茅野市]]にある[[滝]]。'''樽姫の滝'''とも書く『茅野市史 下巻 近現代 民俗』1069 - 1070ページ。『茅野市史 別巻 自然』33ページ。。茅野市指定[[文化財]]。 == 地理 == '''多留姫の滝'''は、茅野市中心市街地の東、[[大泉山]]のふもとを流れる[[柳川 (長野県)|柳川]]に懸かる。大泉山の頂上からは[[富士山]]を望むことができ、その左手に[[八ヶ岳]]がそびえ、眼下に見えるのが多留姫の滝である『長野縣町村誌 中南信編』3351ページ。。まず高さ・幅ともに9メートルで落水し、次いで向きを直角に変え、高さ3メートル・幅1メートルで落水する。『長野縣町村誌』によると、その形は玉簾を掛けたようで、「潺々湲々」と水が流れ落ち、その音はまるで風中の[[琴]]のよう。[[春]]は[[フジ属|藤]]の花に[[ホトトギス]]、[[夏]]は納涼、[[秋]]は[[紅葉]]、[[冬]]は飛[[雪]]と、[[四季]]を通して滝の景色が楽しめるとある。滝のすぐ下流には柳影橋やながれ橋が架かり{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=多留姫文学自然の里歌碑マップ |publisher=多留姫文学自然の里創造委員会 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}、さらに[[長野県道・山梨県道17号茅野北杜韮崎線]]の多留姫大橋が谷をひとまたぎする国土地理院「[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 ウォッちず]」より（2012年5月26日閲覧）。。 滝のそばに'''多留姫神社'''があり、[[建御名方神]]（[[諏訪大社]]の[[祭神]]）の子・[[多満留姫|多留姫神]]（多留の御前）をまつっている『日本歴史地名大系 第20巻 長野県の地名』358ページ。。[[神社]]の[[面積]]は446[[坪]]で、創建の時期は不明だが、少なくとも750年前にはすでに存在していたことを示す文献がある。 茅野市は[[1988年]]（[[昭和]]63年）[[7月29日]]付で多留姫の滝を[[名勝]]（[[滝|瀑布]]）として茅野市指定文化財に指定{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=市指定文化財 |publisher=茅野市 |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}。現在、滝周辺を'''多留姫文学自然の里'''として開放している。地元の中沢区・御作田区・田道区は共同で多留姫文学自然の里創造委員会をつくり、整備や催事の運営を行っている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=多留姫文学自然の里 |publisher=茅野市 |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}。滝周辺には[[松尾芭蕉]]ほか多くの[[歌人]]・[[俳句|俳人]]による作品を刻んだいくつもの[[石碑]]が建立されており、自作の[[歌]]や[[俳句]]を投稿できる投句箱も用意されている。多留姫文学自然の里の[[マスコット]]は'''たるひめちゃん'''で、滝を背景にが滝壺に浮かんだ[[巻物]]に腰掛ける[[キャラクター]]の姿を描く。[[2014年]]（平成26年）度は里の10周年記念行事を催すこととしており、新たに地元の高校生がデザインした[[美少女]]キャラクターの'''紫音'''（しおん、[[フジ (植物)|フジ]]の[[紫|紫色]]と滝の音から命名）をマスコットに起用している{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title= 地域 : 多留姫”萌えキャラ”に　「茅野の創造委」滝周辺の魅力発信へ |newspaper=[[長野日報]] |publisher=長野日報社 |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}。 File:Taruhime Shrine 1.jpg|多留姫神社 File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|柳影橋 File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|ながれ橋 File:Taruhime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|ながれ橋から多留姫の滝を望む File:Taruhime-ohashi bridge.JPG|多留姫大橋 == 伝承 == ;[[椀貸伝説]] :多留姫が村人に[[食器|膳椀]]を必要な分だけ貸してくれる、というもの。『[[長野県史]]』に紹介されているものを要約して以下に記す。 ::むかし、働き者で仲むつまじい[[夫婦]]が[[子供]]を授かった。[[出産]]のお祝い事をしようとしたが、貧しかった彼らは客を迎え入れるだけの数の膳椀を持っていなかった。ある日のこと、土手で休んでいると[[夢]]の中に多留姫が現れ、[[紙]]に必要な膳椀の数を書いて滝壺に落とすよう告げた。夫婦二人とも同じ夢を見たので言うとおりにすると、翌朝には滝壺に膳椀が用意されていた。夫婦はお祝い事をすることができ、使った膳椀を洗って滝壺に返した。この話を聞いた怠け者が、同じような方法を試して膳椀を手に入れた。返すとき、一つくらいなら分からないだろうと思って全部を返さなかったため、以来多留姫は貸すことをやめてしまった『長野県史 民俗編 第二巻（三） 南信地方 ことばと伝承』589 - 591ページ。。 :このほか、貸した膳椀が壊されてしまったために貸さなくなった、という話もある。 [[File:Kuzui-jinja pond.jpg|thumb|葛井神社の池]] [[File:Taruhime Falls upper.jpg|thumb|多留姫十二勝・獅子吼瀑
（多留姫の滝上段）]] ;抜け穴伝説 :多留姫の滝に流した物が別の場所で発見される、というもの。滝壺に椀や[[糠]]を流したところ、それが茅野市ちの上原にある[[葛井神社]]の[[池]]に浮かんでいたという。なお、この葛井神社の池にはさらなる抜け穴伝説が存在しており、諏訪大社[[大晦日]]の[[神事]]のあと、お供え物を投げ込むと[[遠江国]]（[[静岡県]]）にある池に浮かぶとされ、[[諏訪大社七不思議]]の一つに挙げられている『日本歴史地名大系 第20巻 長野県の地名』346 - 347ページ。。 == 多留姫十二勝 == '''多留姫十二勝'''は、多留姫神社周辺にある12か所の選りすぐりの景勝地のことである。[[1902年]]（[[明治]]35年）の神社整備に合わせて選定されたのが始まりで、[[1906年]]（明治39年）に[[小平雪人]]と[[坪井咬菜]]が改めて選定した。多留姫十二勝は以下の通り「多留姫文学自然の里」（パンフレット）より。。 # 機山堂（きざんどう） - [[最澄]]の作といわれている[[仏像]]を安置している場所。 # 待月壇（たいげつだん） - 八ヶ岳から上る[[月]]を眺める場所。 # 古藤径（ことうけい） - 神社から橋へ下る小道。 # 柳影橋（りゅうえいきょう） - 柳川に架かる[[橋]]で、その影が柳川の水面に映ることに由来。 # 甑岩（こしきいわ） - [[乞食]]が寝泊まりしていた「乞食岩」を由来とする[[洞窟|洞穴]]。 # 獅子吼瀑（ししくたき、ししくばく） - 多留姫の滝そのもの。 # 喚魚潭（かんぎょたん） - 多留姫の滝の滝壺。 # 載酒亭（さいしゅてい） - [[四阿]]。滝に[[酒]]を捧げる、もしくは酒を酌み交わす場所。 # 烏巾峰（うきんほう、えぼしいわ） - [[山伏]]や[[天狗]]が頭に被っている黒い[[頭巾]]（=烏巾）に似た巨岩。 # 蛍火台（けいかだい） - 柳川を舞う[[ホタル|蛍]]を眺める場所。現在は[[農薬]]のため蛍は生息していない。 # 望岳丘（ぼうがくきゅう） - 富士山や八ヶ岳などの山々が望める場所。 # 採芝林（さいしりん） - 村人が芝刈りに行く場所。 == アクセス == ;[[公共交通機関]] :多留姫神社から西へ歩くと機山堂があり、これを通過し[[長野県道188号上槻木矢ヶ崎線]]の中沢[[交差点]]に出ると、近くに中沢[[バス停留所|バス停]]がある「[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion 多留姫神社の地図]」より（2012年5月31日閲覧）。。[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央本線]]・[[茅野駅]]から[[路線バス]]の中沢線に乗車、中沢バス停まで20分間弱である茅野市「[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf 平成24年度版 茅野市 バス路線案内 平成24年4月28日改正版]」より（2012年5月31日閲覧）。。 == 多留姫の滝に関連する作品 == * 『玉川村の歌』 - [[作家]]の[[島木赤彦]]による。多留姫の滝が登場する『赤彦全集 第二巻』577 - 580ページ。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 『長野縣町村誌 中南信編』[[郷土出版社]]、[[1985年]]。 * [[島木赤彦|久保田俊彦]] 著『赤彦全集 第二巻』[[岩波書店]]、[[1969年]]。 * 窪田文明 著『信州の滝紀行 名瀑100選』郷土出版社、[[1995年]]。ISBN 4876632928 * 下中邦彦 編『日本歴史地名大系 第20巻 長野県の地名』[[平凡社]]、[[1979年]]。 * 茅野市 編『茅野市史 下巻 近現代 民俗』茅野市、[[1988年]]。 * 茅野市 編『茅野市史 別巻 自然』茅野市、[[1986年]]。 * 長野県 編 『長野県史 民俗編 第二巻（三） 南信地方 ことばと伝承』長野県史刊行会、[[1989年]]。 * 多留姫文学自然の里創造委員会「多留姫文学自然の里」（パンフレット）。 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[滝]] * [[大泉山]] - [[小泉山]] * [[葛井神社]] * [[玉川村 (長野県)]] * [[ゆるキャラ]] - [[萌えおこし]] * [[茅野市#観光スポット|茅野市の観光スポット]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html 茅野市 多留姫文学自然の里] * [https://navi.chinotabi.jp/spot/3115/ 茅野観光ナビ 多留姫の滝] {{諏訪信仰}} {{デフォルトソート:たるひめのたき}} [[Category:中部地方の滝]] [[Category:長野県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:長野県の地形]] [[Category:茅野市の地理]] [[Category:天竜川水系]]

(植物)|フジ]]の[[紫|紫色]]と滝の音から命名）をマスコットに起用している{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title= 地域 : 多留姫”萌えキャラ”に　「茅野の創造委」滝周辺の魅力発信へ |newspaper=[[長野日報]] |publisher=長野日報社 |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}。 File:Taruhime Shrine 1.jpg|多留姫神社 File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|柳影橋 File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|ながれ橋 File:Taruhime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|ながれ橋から多留姫の滝を望む File:Taruhime-ohashi bridge.JPG|多留姫大橋 == 伝承 == ;[[椀貸伝説]] :多留姫が村人に[[食器|膳椀]]を必要な分だけ貸してくれる、というもの。『[[長野県史]]』に紹介されているものを要約して以下に記す。 ::むかし、働き者で仲むつまじい[[夫婦]]が[[子供]]を授かった。[[出産]]のお祝い事をしようとしたが、貧しかった彼らは客を迎え入れるだけの数の膳椀を持っていなかった。ある日のこと、土手で休んでいると[[夢]]の中に多留姫が現れ、[[紙]]に必要な膳椀の数を書いて滝壺に落とすよう告げた。夫婦二人とも同じ夢を見たので言うとおりにすると、翌朝には滝壺に膳椀が用意されていた。夫婦はお祝い事をすることができ、使った膳椀を洗って滝壺に返した。この話を聞いた怠け者が、同じような方法を試して膳椀を手に入れた。返すとき、一つくらいなら分からないだろうと思って全部を返さなかったため、以来多留姫は貸すことをやめてしまった『長野県史 民俗編 第二巻（三） 南信地方 ことばと伝承』589 - 591ページ。。 :このほか、貸した膳椀が壊されてしまったために貸さなくなった、という話もある。 [[File:Kuzui-jinja pond.jpg|thumb|葛井神社の池]] [[File:Taruhime Falls upper.jpg|thumb|多留姫十二勝・獅子吼瀑
（多留姫の滝上段）]] ;抜け穴伝説 :多留姫の滝に流した物が別の場所で発見される、というもの。滝壺に椀や[[糠]]を流したところ、それが茅野市ちの上原にある[[葛井神社]]の[[池]]に浮かんでいたという。なお、この葛井神社の池にはさらなる抜け穴伝説が存在しており、諏訪大社[[大晦日]]の[[神事]]のあと、お供え物を投げ込むと[[遠江国]]（[[静岡県]]）にある池に浮かぶとされ、[[諏訪大社七不思議]]の一つに挙げられている『日本歴史地名大系 第20巻 長野県の地名』346 - 347ページ。。 == 多留姫十二勝 == '''多留姫十二勝'''は、多留姫神社周辺にある12か所の選りすぐりの景勝地のことである。[[1902年]]（[[明治]]35年）の神社整備に合わせて選定されたのが始まりで、[[1906年]]（明治39年）に[[小平雪人]]と[[坪井咬菜]]が改めて選定した。多留姫十二勝は以下の通り「多留姫文学自然の里」（パンフレット）より。。 # 機山堂（きざんどう） - [[最澄]]の作といわれている[[仏像]]を安置している場所。 # 待月壇（たいげつだん） - 八ヶ岳から上る[[月]]を眺める場所。 # 古藤径（ことうけい） - 神社から橋へ下る小道。 # 柳影橋（りゅうえいきょう） - 柳川に架かる[[橋]]で、その影が柳川の水面に映ることに由来。 # 甑岩（こしきいわ） - [[乞食]]が寝泊まりしていた「乞食岩」を由来とする[[洞窟|洞穴]]。 # 獅子吼瀑（ししくたき、ししくばく） - 多留姫の滝そのもの。 # 喚魚潭（かんぎょたん） - 多留姫の滝の滝壺。 # 載酒亭（さいしゅてい） - [[四阿]]。滝に[[酒]]を捧げる、もしくは酒を酌み交わす場所。 # 烏巾峰（うきんほう、えぼしいわ） - [[山伏]]や[[天狗]]が頭に被っている黒い[[頭巾]]（=烏巾）に似た巨岩。 # 蛍火台（けいかだい） - 柳川を舞う[[ホタル|蛍]]を眺める場所。現在は[[農薬]]のため蛍は生息していない。 # 望岳丘（ぼうがくきゅう） - 富士山や八ヶ岳などの山々が望める場所。 # 採芝林（さいしりん） - 村人が芝刈りに行く場所。 == アクセス == ;[[公共交通機関]] :多留姫神社から西へ歩くと機山堂があり、これを通過し[[長野県道188号上槻木矢ヶ崎線]]の中沢[[交差点]]に出ると、近くに中沢[[バス停留所|バス停]]がある「[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion 多留姫神社の地図]」より（2012年5月31日閲覧）。。[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央本線]]・[[茅野駅]]から[[路線バス]]の中沢線に乗車、中沢バス停まで20分間弱である茅野市「[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf 平成24年度版 茅野市 バス路線案内 平成24年4月28日改正版]」より（2012年5月31日閲覧）。。 == 多留姫の滝に関連する作品 == * 『玉川村の歌』 - [[作家]]の[[島木赤彦]]による。多留姫の滝が登場する『赤彦全集 第二巻』577 - 580ページ。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 『長野縣町村誌 中南信編』[[郷土出版社]]、[[1985年]]。 * [[島木赤彦|久保田俊彦]] 著『赤彦全集 第二巻』[[岩波書店]]、[[1969年]]。 * 窪田文明 著『信州の滝紀行 名瀑100選』郷土出版社、[[1995年]]。ISBN 4876632928 * 下中邦彦 編『日本歴史地名大系 第20巻 長野県の地名』[[平凡社]]、[[1979年]]。 * 茅野市 編『茅野市史 下巻 近現代 民俗』茅野市、[[1988年]]。 * 茅野市 編『茅野市史 別巻 自然』茅野市、[[1986年]]。 * 長野県 編 『長野県史 民俗編 第二巻（三） 南信地方 ことばと伝承』長野県史刊行会、[[1989年]]。 * 多留姫文学自然の里創造委員会「多留姫文学自然の里」（パンフレット）。 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[滝]] * [[大泉山]] - [[小泉山]] * [[葛井神社]] * [[玉川村 (長野県)]] * [[ゆるキャラ]] - [[萌えおこし]] * [[茅野市#観光スポット|茅野市の観光スポット]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html 茅野市 多留姫文学自然の里] * [https://navi.chinotabi.jp/spot/3115/ 茅野観光ナビ 多留姫の滝] {{諏訪信仰}} {{デフォルトソート:たるひめのたき}} [[Category:中部地方の滝]] [[Category:長野県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:長野県の地形]] [[Category:茅野市の地理]] [[Category:天竜川水系]]

{{Infobox Waterfall |name = Taruhime Falls (多留姫の滝, 樽姫の滝) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Taruhime Falls view from Ryuei Bridge.jpg|300px|Taruhime Falls]] |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|36.5|N|138|12|56|E|type:landmark_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012). |location = [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Chino City]] Tamagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 120. |height = 12 |width = 9 |watercourse = [[Tenryu River]] system, Yanagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 140. |osm_zoom = 10 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Taruhime Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Taruhime Shrine 2.jpg|300px|Taruhime Shrine Main Hall]]
Main Hall |location = Nagano Prefecture, Chino City, Tamagawa"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20214/L0968824/ Mapion Directory: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|35.5|N|138|12|53.5|E}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21486113884&latitude=35.993205205672 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |deity = [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] |Ranking = |established = |main_hall = |alternate_name = |pilgrimage = |festival = |rituals = }} '''Taruhime Falls''' (多留姫の滝, also 樽姫の滝) is a waterfall in [[Chino, Nagano|Chino City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as "Taruhime Falls" in alternate kanji‘Chino City History: Modern and Folklore,’ pp. 1069–1070.‘Chino City History: Supplement Natural History,’ p. 33.. The falls are designated as a cultural property by Chino City. == Geography == '''Taruhime Falls''' is located east of Chino City's central area, at the foot of [[Mount Oizumi]], along the [[Yanagawa (Nagano)|Yanagawa River]]. From the summit of Mount Oizumi, one can see [[Mount Fuji]] to the left of [[Mount Yatsugatake]], with the falls visible below‘Nagano Prefectural Town and Village Records: Chuo and Minami-Shin Regions,’ p. 3351.. The falls have an initial drop of 9 meters in height and width, followed by a right-angle turn with a 3-meter height and 1-meter width. The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[autumn leaves]], and [[snow]] in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Taruhime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Taruhime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaruhime|Taruhime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Taruhime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Taruhime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Taruhime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Taruhime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Taruhime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Taruhime Shrine 1.jpg|Taruhime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Taruhime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Taruhime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Taruhime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft}} : A legend tells of Taruhime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Taruhime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Taruhime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Taruhime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Taruhime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Taruhime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from [[JR East]]'s [[Chuo Main Line]] [[Chino Station]], reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Taruhime's Waterfall]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[sn:Taruhime's Waterfall]] [[en:Taruhime's Waterfall]] {{translated page|ja|多留姫の滝|version=97968335|comment=Imported full ja history}}
