Chino City designated Tamaru-hime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[:en:Matsuo Basho|Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Tamaru-hime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Tamaru-hime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Tamaru-hime Shrine 1.jpg|Tamaru-hime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Tamaru-hime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Tamaru-hime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lending Bowl Legend|qq=}} : A legend tells of Tamaru-hime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural History|1=Nagano Prefectural History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184907}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Tamaru-hime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Tamaru-hime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Tamaru-hime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[:en:Public Transportation|Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Tamaru-hime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Tamaru-hime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]]'s [[:en:Chuo Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] [[:en:Chino Station|Chino Station]], reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|多留姫の滝|version=97968335|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] {{Commons category|Taruhime Falls}} {{wikidata link|Q11430989}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Chino, Nagano]] [[Category:Landforms of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Tamaru-hime's Waterfall]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Tenryu River]] [[Category:Waterfalls of the Chubu region]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[commons:Category:Taruhime Falls]][[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[commons:Category:Taruhime Falls]][[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[sn:commons:Category:Taruhime Falls]][[ja:多留姫の滝]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11430989]] Wikidata name: Taruhime Falls [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Waterfall |name = Tamaru-hime Falls (多留姫の滝, 樽姫の滝) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tamaru-hime Falls view from Ryuei Bridge.jpg|300px|Tamaru-hime Falls]] |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|36.5|N|138|12|56|E|type:landmark_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012). |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[Chino City]] Tamagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 120. |height = 12 |width = 9 |watercourse = [[Tenryu River]] system, Yanagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 140. |osm_zoom = 10 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tamaru-hime Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tamaru-hime Shrine 2.jpg|300px|Tamaru-hime Shrine Main Hall]]
Main Hall |location = Nagano Prefecture, Chino City, Tamagawa"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20214/L0968824/ Mapion Directory: Tamaru-hime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|35.5|N|138|12|53.5|E}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21486113884&latitude=35.993205205672 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |deity = [[Tamaru-hime|Tamaru-hime-no-Kami]] |Ranking = |established = |main_hall = |alternate_name = |pilgrimage = |festival = |rituals = }} '''Tamaru-hime Falls''' (多留姫の滝, also 樽姫の滝) is a waterfall in [[:en:Chino, Nagano|Chino City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as "Tamaru-hime Falls" in alternate kanji‘Chino City History: Modern and Folklore,’ pp. 1069–1070.‘Chino City History: Supplement Natural History,’ p. 33.. The falls are designated as a cultural property by Chino City. == Geography == '''Tamaru-hime Falls''' is located east of Chino City's central area, at the foot of [[Mount Oizumi]], along the [[Yanagawa (Nagano)|Yanagawa River]]. From the summit of Mount Oizumi, one can see [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] to the left of [[:en:Mount Yatsugatake|Mount Yatsugatake]], with the falls visible below‘Nagano Prefectural Town and Village Records: Chuo and Minami-Shin Regions,’ p. 3351.. The falls have an initial drop of 9 meters in height and width, followed by a right-angle turn with a 3-meter height and 1-meter width. The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[:en:cuckoos|cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[:en:autumn leaves|autumn leaves]], and [[:en:Snow|Snow]] in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Tamaru-hime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Tamaru-hime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaru-hime|Tamaru-hime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Tamaru-hime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[:en:Matsuo Basho|Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Tamaru-hime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Tamaru-hime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Tamaru-hime Shrine 1.jpg|Tamaru-hime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Tamaru-hime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Tamaru-hime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lending Bowl Legend|qq=}} : A legend tells of Tamaru-hime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural History|1=Nagano Prefectural History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184907}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Tamaru-hime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Tamaru-hime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Tamaru-hime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[:en:Public Transportation|Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Tamaru-hime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Tamaru-hime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]]'s [[:en:Chuo Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] [[:en:Chino Station|Chino Station]], reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|多留姫の滝|version=97968335|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] {{Commons category|Taruhime Falls}} {{wikidata link|Q11430989}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Chino, Nagano]] [[Category:Landforms of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Tamaru-hime's Waterfall]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Tenryu River]] [[Category:Waterfalls of the Chubu region]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[:en:cuckoos|cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[:en:autumn leaves|autumn leaves]], and {{ill|Snow|en|Snow|WD=Q7561|lt=Snow}} in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Tamaru-hime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Tamaru-hime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaru-hime|Tamaru-hime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Tamaru-hime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[:en:Matsuo Basho|Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Tamaru-hime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Tamaru-hime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Tamaru-hime Shrine 1.jpg|Tamaru-hime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Tamaru-hime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Tamaru-hime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lending Bowl Legend|qq=}} : A legend tells of Tamaru-hime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural History|1=Nagano Prefectural History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184907}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Tamaru-hime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Tamaru-hime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Tamaru-hime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[:en:Public Transportation|Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Tamaru-hime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Tamaru-hime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}'s [[:en:Chuo Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] {{ill|Chino Station|en|Chino Station|WD=Q844063|lt=Chino Station}}, reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|多留姫の滝|version=97968335|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] {{Commons category|Taruhime Falls}} {{wikidata link|Q11430989}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Chino, Nagano]] [[Category:Landforms of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Tamaru-hime's Waterfall]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Tenryu River]] [[Category:Waterfalls of the Chubu region]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Taruhime Falls]][[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[co:commons:Category:Taruhime Falls]][[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[sn:commons:Category:Taruhime Falls]][[ja:多留姫の滝]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11430989]] Wikidata name: Taruhime Falls [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Waterfall |name = Tamaru-hime Falls (多留姫の滝, 樽姫の滝) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tamaru-hime Falls view from Ryuei Bridge.jpg|300px|Tamaru-hime Falls]] |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|36.5|N|138|12|56|E|type:landmark_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012). |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, [[Chino City]] Tamagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 120. |height = 12 |width = 9 |watercourse = [[Tenryu River]] system, Yanagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 140. |osm_zoom = 10 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tamaru-hime Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tamaru-hime Shrine 2.jpg|300px|Tamaru-hime Shrine Main Hall]]
Main Hall |location = Nagano Prefecture, Chino City, Tamagawa"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20214/L0968824/ Mapion Directory: Tamaru-hime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|35.5|N|138|12|53.5|E}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21486113884&latitude=35.993205205672 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |deity = [[Tamaru-hime|Tamaru-hime-no-Kami]] |Ranking = |established = |main_hall = |alternate_name = |pilgrimage = |festival = |rituals = }} '''Tamaru-hime Falls''' (多留姫の滝, also 樽姫の滝) is a waterfall in {{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|WD=Q838660|lt=Chino City}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is also referred to as "Tamaru-hime Falls" in alternate kanji‘Chino City History: Modern and Folklore,’ pp. 1069–1070.‘Chino City History: Supplement Natural History,’ p. 33.. The falls are designated as a cultural property by Chino City. == Geography == '''Tamaru-hime Falls''' is located east of Chino City's central area, at the foot of [[Mount Oizumi]], along the [[Yanagawa (Nagano)|Yanagawa River]]. From the summit of Mount Oizumi, one can see {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|WD=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji}} to the left of [[:en:Mount Yatsugatake|Mount Yatsugatake]], with the falls visible below‘Nagano Prefectural Town and Village Records: Chuo and Minami-Shin Regions,’ p. 3351.. The falls have an initial drop of 9 meters in height and width, followed by a right-angle turn with a 3-meter height and 1-meter width. The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[:en:cuckoos|cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[:en:autumn leaves|autumn leaves]], and {{ill|Snow|en|Snow|WD=Q7561|lt=Snow}} in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Tamaru-hime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Tamaru-hime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaru-hime|Tamaru-hime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Tamaru-hime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[:en:Matsuo Basho|Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Tamaru-hime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Tamaru-hime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Tamaru-hime Shrine 1.jpg|Tamaru-hime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Tamaru-hime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Tamaru-hime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lending Bowl Legend|qq=}} : A legend tells of Tamaru-hime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural History|1=Nagano Prefectural History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184907}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Tamaru-hime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Tamaru-hime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Tamaru-hime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[:en:Public Transportation|Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Tamaru-hime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Tamaru-hime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}'s [[:en:Chuo Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] {{ill|Chino Station|en|Chino Station|WD=Q844063|lt=Chino Station}}, reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|多留姫の滝|version=97968335|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] {{Commons category|Taruhime Falls}} {{wikidata link|Q11430989}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Chino, Nagano]] [[Category:Landforms of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Tamaru-hime's Waterfall]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Tenryu River]] [[Category:Waterfalls of the Chubu region]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Taruhime Falls]][[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[co:commons:Category:Taruhime Falls]][[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[sn:commons:Category:Taruhime Falls]][[ja:多留姫の滝]] {{Infobox Waterfall |name = Tamaru-hime Falls (多留姫の滝, 樽姫の滝) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tamaru-hime Falls view from Ryuei Bridge.jpg|300px|Tamaru-hime Falls]] |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|36.5|N|138|12|56|E|type:landmark_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012). |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, [[Chino City]] Tamagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 120. |height = 12 |width = 9 |watercourse = [[Tenryu River]] system, Yanagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 140. |osm_zoom = 10 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tamaru-hime Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tamaru-hime Shrine 2.jpg|300px|Tamaru-hime Shrine Main Hall]]
Main Hall |location = Nagano Prefecture, Chino City, Tamagawa"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20214/L0968824/ Mapion Directory: Tamaru-hime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|35.5|N|138|12|53.5|E}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21486113884&latitude=35.993205205672 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |deity = [[Tamaru-hime|Tamaru-hime-no-Kami]] |Ranking = |established = |main_hall = |alternate_name = |pilgrimage = |festival = |rituals = }} '''Tamaru-hime Falls''' (多留姫の滝, also 樽姫の滝) is a waterfall in {{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|WD=Q838660|lt=Chino City}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is also referred to as "Tamaru-hime Falls" in alternate kanji‘Chino City History: Modern and Folklore,’ pp. 1069–1070.‘Chino City History: Supplement Natural History,’ p. 33.. The falls are designated as a cultural property by Chino City. == Geography == '''Tamaru-hime Falls''' is located east of Chino City's central area, at the foot of [[Mount Oizumi]], along the [[Yanagawa (Nagano)|Yanagawa River]]. From the summit of Mount Oizumi, one can see {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|WD=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji}} to the left of [[:en:Mount Yatsugatake|Mount Yatsugatake]], with the falls visible below‘Nagano Prefectural Town and Village Records: Chuo and Minami-Shin Regions,’ p. 3351.. The falls have an initial drop of 9 meters in height and width, followed by a right-angle turn with a 3-meter height and 1-meter width. The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[:en:cuckoos|cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[:en:autumn leaves|autumn leaves]], and {{ill|Snow|en|Snow|WD=Q7561|lt=Snow}} in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Tamaru-hime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Tamaru-hime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaru-hime|Tamaru-hime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Tamaru-hime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[:en:Matsuo Basho|Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Tamaru-hime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Tamaru-hime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Tamaru-hime Shrine 1.jpg|Tamaru-hime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Tamaru-hime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Tamaru-hime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lending Bowl Legend|qq=}} : A legend tells of Tamaru-hime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural History|1=Nagano Prefectural History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184907}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Tamaru-hime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Tamaru-hime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Tamaru-hime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[:en:Public Transportation|Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Tamaru-hime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Tamaru-hime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}}'s [[:en:Chuo Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] {{ill|Chino Station|en|Chino Station|WD=Q844063|lt=Chino Station}}, reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|多留姫の滝|version=97968335|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] {{Commons category|Taruhime Falls}} {{wikidata link|Q11430989}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Chino, Nagano]] [[Category:Landforms of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Tamaru-hime's Waterfall]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Tenryu River]] [[Category:Waterfalls of the Chubu region]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Take the Nakazawa Line bus from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}'s [[:en:Chuo Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] {{ill|Chino Station|en|Chino Station|WD=Q844063|lt=Chino Station|1=Chino Station}}, reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|多留姫の滝|version=97968335|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] {{Commons category|Taruhime Falls}} {{wikidata link|Q11430989}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Chino, Nagano]] [[Category:Landforms of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Tamaru-hime's Waterfall]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Tenryu River]] [[Category:Waterfalls of the Chubu region]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Taruhime Falls]][[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[co:commons:Category:Taruhime Falls]][[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Taruhime Falls]][[ja:多留姫の滝]] {{Infobox Waterfall |name = Tamaru-hime Falls (多留姫の滝, 樽姫の滝) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tamaru-hime Falls view from Ryuei Bridge.jpg|300px|Tamaru-hime Falls]] |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|36.5|N|138|12|56|E|type:landmark_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012). |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, [[Chino City]] Tamagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 120. |height = 12 |width = 9 |watercourse = [[Tenryu River]] system, Yanagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 140. |osm_zoom = 10 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tamaru-hime Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tamaru-hime Shrine 2.jpg|300px|Tamaru-hime Shrine Main Hall]]
Main Hall |location = Nagano Prefecture, Chino City, Tamagawa"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20214/L0968824/ Mapion Directory: Tamaru-hime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|35.5|N|138|12|53.5|E}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21486113884&latitude=35.993205205672 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |deity = [[Tamaru-hime|Tamaru-hime-no-Kami]] |Ranking = |established = |main_hall = |alternate_name = |pilgrimage = |festival = |rituals = }} '''Tamaru-hime Falls''' (多留姫の滝, also 樽姫の滝) is a waterfall in {{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|WD=Q838660|lt=Chino City|1=Chino, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is also referred to as "Tamaru-hime Falls" in alternate kanji‘Chino City History: Modern and Folklore,’ pp. 1069–1070.‘Chino City History: Supplement Natural History,’ p. 33.. The falls are designated as a cultural property by Chino City. == Geography == '''Tamaru-hime Falls''' is located east of Chino City's central area, at the foot of [[Mount Oizumi]], along the [[Yanagawa (Nagano)|Yanagawa River]]. From the summit of Mount Oizumi, one can see {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|WD=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}} to the left of [[:en:Mount Yatsugatake|Mount Yatsugatake]], with the falls visible below‘Nagano Prefectural Town and Village Records: Chuo and Minami-Shin Regions,’ p. 3351.. The falls have an initial drop of 9 meters in height and width, followed by a right-angle turn with a 3-meter height and 1-meter width. The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[:en:cuckoos|cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[:en:autumn leaves|autumn leaves]], and {{ill|Snow|en|Snow|WD=Q7561|lt=Snow|1=Snow}} in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Tamaru-hime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Tamaru-hime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaru-hime|Tamaru-hime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Tamaru-hime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[:en:Matsuo Basho|Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Tamaru-hime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Tamaru-hime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Tamaru-hime Shrine 1.jpg|Tamaru-hime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Tamaru-hime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Tamaru-hime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lending Bowl Legend|qq=}} : A legend tells of Tamaru-hime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural History|1=Q97184907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184907}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Tamaru-hime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Tamaru-hime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Tamaru-hime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[:en:Public Transportation|Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Tamaru-hime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Tamaru-hime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}'s [[:en:Chuo Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] {{ill|Chino Station|en|Chino Station|WD=Q844063|lt=Chino Station|1=Chino Station}}, reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|多留姫の滝|version=97968335|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] {{Commons category|Taruhime Falls}} {{wikidata link|Q11430989}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Geography of Chino, Nagano]] [[Category:Landforms of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Tamaru-hime's Waterfall]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Tenryu River]] [[Category:Waterfalls of the Chubu region]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The park's mascot is '''Tamaru-hime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Tamaru-hime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Tamaru-hime Shrine 1.jpg|Tamaru-hime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Tamaru-hime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Tamaru-hime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lending Bowl Legend|qq=}} : A legend tells of Tamaru-hime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural History|1=Q97184907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184907}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Tamaru-hime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Tamaru-hime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Tamaru-hime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[:en:Public Transportation|Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Tamaru-hime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Tamaru-hime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}'s [[:en:Chuo Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] {{ill|Chino Station|en|Chino Station|WD=Q844063|lt=Chino Station|1=Chino Station}}, reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|多留姫の滝|version=97968335|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] {{Commons category|Taruhime Falls}} {{wikidata link|Q11430989}} [[Category:Geography of Chino, Nagano]] [[Category:Landforms of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Tamaru-hime's Waterfall]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Tenryu River]] [[Category:Waterfalls of the Chubu region]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Taruhime Falls]][[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[co:commons:Category:Taruhime Falls]][[ja:多留姫の滝]] [[ja:多留姫の滝]] {{Infobox Waterfall |name = Tamaru-hime Falls (多留姫の滝, 樽姫の滝) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tamaru-hime Falls view from Ryuei Bridge.jpg|300px|Tamaru-hime Falls]] |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|36.5|N|138|12|56|E|type:landmark_region:JP-20|display=inline,title}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012). |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, [[Chino City]] Tamagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 120. |height = 12 |width = 9 |watercourse = [[Tenryu River]] system, Yanagawa‘Shinshu Waterfall Journey: 100 Selected Famous Waterfalls,’ p. 140. |osm_zoom = 10 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tamaru-hime Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tamaru-hime Shrine 2.jpg|300px|Tamaru-hime Shrine Main Hall]]
Main Hall |location = Nagano Prefecture, Chino City, Tamagawa"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/phonebook/M06005/20214/L0968824/ Mapion Directory: Tamaru-hime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |coordinates = {{Coord|35|59|35.5|N|138|12|53.5|E}}Coordinates obtained from "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21486113884&latitude=35.993205205672 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 31, 2012). |deity = [[Tamaru-hime|Tamaru-hime-no-Kami]] |Ranking = |established = |main_hall = |alternate_name = |pilgrimage = |festival = |rituals = }} '''Tamaru-hime Falls''' (多留姫の滝, also 樽姫の滝) is a waterfall in {{ill|Chino, Nagano|en|Chino, Nagano|WD=Q838660|lt=Chino City|1=Chino, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is also referred to as "Tamaru-hime Falls" in alternate kanji‘Chino City History: Modern and Folklore,’ pp. 1069–1070.‘Chino City History: Supplement Natural History,’ p. 33.. The falls are designated as a cultural property by Chino City. == Geography == '''Tamaru-hime Falls''' is located east of Chino City's central area, at the foot of [[Mount Oizumi]], along the [[Yanagawa (Nagano)|Yanagawa River]]. From the summit of Mount Oizumi, one can see {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|WD=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}} to the left of [[:en:Mount Yatsugatake|Mount Yatsugatake]], with the falls visible below‘Nagano Prefectural Town and Village Records: Chuo and Minami-Shin Regions,’ p. 3351.. The falls have an initial drop of 9 meters in height and width, followed by a right-angle turn with a 3-meter height and 1-meter width. The records describe the water flow as resembling a beaded curtain, producing a sound akin to a harp in the wind. Across the seasons, visitors can enjoy [[wisteria]] and [[:en:cuckoos|cuckoos]] in spring, cool retreats in summer, [[:en:autumn leaves|autumn leaves]], and {{ill|Snow|en|Snow|WD=Q7561|lt=Snow|1=Snow}} in winter. Downstream, the Yanakage and Nagare Bridges span the falls{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/3786.pdf |title=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park Monument Map |publisher=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park Creation Committee |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}, while the Tamaru-hime Bridge of [[Nagano Prefectural Route 17]] crosses the valleyGeospatial Information Authority of Japan "[http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=138.21555558329&latitude=35.993490818409 Watchizu]" (Accessed May 26, 2012).. Next to the falls is '''Tamaru-hime Shrine''', dedicated to [[Tamaru-hime|Tamaru-hime-no-Kami]] (daughter of [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata-no-Kami]], the deity of [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]])‘Japanese Historical Place Names: Volume 20, Nagano Prefecture,’ p. 358.. While the shrine's founding date is unknown, it is documented to have existed over 750 years ago. Chino City designated Tamaru-hime Falls as a cultural property on [[July 29, 1988]] (Showa 63){{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/1665.html |title=City Designated Cultural Properties |publisher=Chino City |date=2018-12-03 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. The surrounding area, known as '''Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park''', is managed by a committee from local districts that maintains and hosts events{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.chino.lg.jp/soshiki/shogaigakushu/851.html |title=Tamaru-hime Literature and Nature Park |publisher=Chino City |date=2019-12-20 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. Various monuments inscribed with poems and haiku by notable figures, such as [[:en:Matsuo Basho|Matsuo Basho]], are found in the area, along with submission boxes for original works. The park's mascot is '''Tamaru-hime-chan''', depicted sitting on a scroll in the falls' basin. In 2014, to commemorate the park's 10th anniversary, a new mascot named '''Shion''' (紫音) was introduced, designed by local high school students{{Cite news |url=http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140517123128/http://www.nagano-np.co.jp/modules/news/article.php?storyid=31134 |title=Local Charm: Tamaru-hime Character Introduced by Chino Creation Committee to Promote the Falls |newspaper=[[Nagano Daily News]] |publisher=Nagano Newspaper |date=2014-04-18 |archivedate=2014-05-17 |accessdate=2020-01-03 }}. File:Tamaru-hime Shrine 1.jpg|Tamaru-hime Shrine File:Ryuei Bridge.jpg|Yanakage Bridge File:Nagare Bridge.jpg|Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime Falls and Yana River view from Nagare Bridge.jpg|View of Tamaru-hime Falls from Nagare Bridge File:Tamaru-hime-ohashi bridge.JPG|Tamaru-hime Bridge == Legends == ;{{ill|Lending Bowl Legend|ja|椀貸伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lending Bowl Legend|qq=}} : A legend tells of Tamaru-hime lending bowls to villagers in need. The following summary is derived from ‘{{ill|Nagano Prefectural History|ja|長野県史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefectural History|1=Q97184907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184907}}’: :: Long ago, a hardworking couple with a harmonious relationship was blessed with a child. Wanting to celebrate but unable to afford sufficient bowls for the guests, they dreamed of Tamaru-hime instructing them to write their needs on paper and drop it into the falls' basin. Upon doing so, the bowls appeared the next morning. After the celebration, the couple cleaned the bowls and returned them. However, a lazy villager, hearing of this, tried the same but kept one bowl, believing it wouldn't be noticed. Since then, Tamaru-hime ceased lending‘Nagano Prefectural History: Folklore Edition, Volume II (3),’ pp. 589–591.. : Another version suggests that broken bowls caused Tamaru-hime to stop lending. == Access == ;[[:en:Public Transportation|Public Transportation]] : A short walk west from Tamaru-hime Shrine leads to Kizandō. Proceeding further brings you to [[Nagano Prefectural Route 188]] at Nakazawa Intersection, near Nakazawa Bus Stop"[http://www.mapion.co.jp/m/35.9901438_138.2181119_8/v=m5:%E5%A4%9A%E7%95%99%E5%A7%AB%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Mapion Map: Tamaru-hime Shrine]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. Take the Nakazawa Line bus from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}}'s [[:en:Chuo Main Line|Chuo Main Line]] {{ill|Chino Station|en|Chino Station|WD=Q844063|lt=Chino Station|1=Chino Station}}, reaching Nakazawa in under 20 minutesChino City "[http://www.city.chino.lg.jp/kbn/Files/1/01130262/attach/basurosenH24.4.28.pdf Chino City Bus Routes: Revised April 28, 2012]" (Accessed May 31, 2012).. ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|多留姫の滝|version=97968335|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:多留姫の滝]] {{Commons category|Taruhime Falls}} {{wikidata link|Q11430989}} [[Category:Geography of Chino, Nagano]] [[Category:Landforms of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Sites of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Tamaru-hime's Waterfall]] [[Category:Tributaries of the Tenryu River]] [[Category:Waterfalls of the Chubu region]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
